$comment: Each entry pins a repository to an exact commit for reproducible scanning. toolVersion is the npm spec passed to `npx --yes <toolVersion>`. scanSubpath scans only part of a monorepo when set. isStressCase marks entries chosen because the scanner is expected to score them poorly or surprisingly — see METHODOLOGY.md. Never edit an existing entry's commit in place: add a new entry or a dated note instead.

entries.name: harness-score
entries.notes: The scanner scanning itself. Dogfoods L4 on every commit to main — the strongest available ceiling anchor.

entries.name: fakeflix
entries.notes: Known-good anchor from harness-score's own v0.1.2 real-world test — extensive .cursor/rules, skills, and subagents.

entries.name: execa
entries.notes: Excellently engineered library (tests, CI, strict types, lint) with zero AI-harness artifacts. Tests whether strong Sensors/CI/Hygiene alone can be mistaken for harness maturity.

entries.name: octocat-hello-world
entries.notes: GitHub's canonical minimal repository — a README and nothing else. Expected floor: L0.

entries.name: anthropic-cookbook
entries.notes: Anthropic's official cookbook of Claude usage patterns and integration recipes. Tests whether a lab's own example repository carries agent-harness infrastructure or is purely reference code.

entries.name: anthropic-skills
entries.notes: Anthropic's own showcase of Claude Skills — directly on-topic for the artifact-governance and harness-engineering-exemplar angle of this study; if any repo should score well on Skills & Commands, it is this one.

entries.name: openai-cookbook
entries.notes: KNOWN LIMITATION, not yet scanned: checkout fails on Windows — examples/data/hotel_invoices/ contains directory names with a trailing space (e.g. 'extracted_invoice_json /'), which NTFS path-safety (git's core.protectNTFS) rejects. Not a harness-score bug; a git-for-Windows checkout limitation on this specific upstream repo. OpenAI is still represented in the corpus via openai/evals. Revisit from a POSIX host, or with a sparse-checkout excluding that subtree.

entries.name: smolagents
entries.notes: Hugging Face's lightweight agent-building framework. Tests whether an agent framework's own repository is itself agent-harnessed, or just ships agent-building code for others.

entries.name: continue
entries.notes: Continue — open-source AI code assistant / IDE extension. AI-first developer tool; also a natural home for testing whether a Continue-centric repository is legible to a scanner with Cursor as the flagship example.

entries.name: cline
entries.notes: Cline — open-source autonomous coding agent for VS Code, with a large, active contributor base.

entries.name: openhands
entries.notes: OpenHands (formerly OpenDevin) — open-source autonomous software engineering agent platform.

entries.name: goose
entries.notes: Goose — Block's open-source, extensible AI agent for building software autonomously.

entries.name: opencode
entries.notes: opencode — SST's open-source, terminal-based AI coding agent. Default branch is dev, not main.

entries.name: aider
entries.notes: Aider — widely used open-source AI pair-programming CLI, one of the most established agent-native coding tools.

entries.name: zed
entries.notes: Zed — high-performance, AI-integrated code editor. Deliberately large Rust monorepo, scanned whole (no subpath): primary large-monorepo stress case for scan truncation (MAX_FILES) and signal dilution.

entries.name: promptfoo
entries.notes: promptfoo — open-source LLM prompt testing and evaluation framework. Core prompt/eval-engineering entry.

entries.name: openai-evals
entries.notes: OpenAI's open-source framework for evaluating LLMs and LLM systems.

entries.name: dspy
entries.notes: DSPy — Stanford's framework for programming (not prompting) language models, with built-in optimization and evaluation loops.

entries.name: langfuse
entries.notes: Langfuse — open-source LLM observability and evaluation platform. Eval-first stress case: tests the model's blindness to AI-specific sensors (eval suites, LLM-as-judge configs) that today earn zero credit.

entries.name: mcp-servers
entries.notes: The official Model Context Protocol servers collection — directly on-topic for the MCP / artifact-governance angle of this study.

entries.name: awesome-cursorrules
entries.notes: A widely-starred curated collection of example .cursorrules/rules. Deliberate inflation stress case: tests whether harness artifacts vendored under example/template directories are wrongly counted as the repository's own harness.
